terminals/vt52-historical.analysis: DEC VT52 (1975) was manufactured by Digital Equipment Corporation (DEC). First DEC video terminal with cursor addressing. This is a historical reference entry — no automated probe data is available for this terminal.

terminals/vt100-historical.analysis: DEC VT100 (1978) was manufactured by Digital Equipment Corporation (DEC). Defined the escape sequence grammar used by every modern terminal. This is a historical reference entry — no automated probe data is available for this terminal.

terminals/vt220-historical.analysis: DEC VT220 (1983) was manufactured by Digital Equipment Corporation (DEC). Introduced editing operations (ICH/DCH/IL/DL) used by every TUI application. This is a historical reference entry — no automated probe data is available for this terminal.

terminals/xterm-historical.analysis: xterm (1984) was manufactured by MIT / Thomas Dickey. De facto reference implementation — defined most 'standard' terminal behavior. This is a historical reference entry — no automated probe data is available for this terminal.

terminals/vt510-historical.analysis: DEC VT510 (1993) was manufactured by Digital Equipment Corporation (DEC). Reference manual remains the most cited terminal specification. This is a historical reference entry — no automated probe data is available for this terminal.

baseline/core.analysis: 1 of 12 tested terminals achieve 100% Core TUI compliance. Lagging: vt100.js (77%), Terminal.app (91%), Warp (91%). Most commonly missing: TBC clear tab stop (CSI g), CHT forward tab (CSI I), CBT backward tab (CSI Z).

baseline/modern.analysis: 2 of 12 tested terminals achieve 100% Modern TUI compliance. Lagging: vt100.js (27%), xterm.js (78%), Alacritty (86%). Most commonly missing: Mode 2031 color scheme reporting, Color palette (OSC 4), Cursor color (OSC 12).

baseline/rich.analysis: 0 of 12 tested terminals achieve 100% Rich TUI compliance. Lagging: vt100.js (32%), xterm.js (49%), Alacritty (50%). Most commonly missing: iTerm2 Cell Size Reporting (OSC 1337), iTerm2 Capability Reporting (OSC 1337), OSC 5522 advanced clipboard.

baseline/legacy.analysis: 0 of 12 tested terminals achieve 100% Legacy compliance. Lagging: vterm.js (11%), xterm.js (11%), vt100.js (22%). Most commonly missing: DECRQCRA — checksum rectangular area, DECFRA — fill rectangular area, DECERA — erase rectangular area.

baseline/unicode.analysis: 3 of 12 tested terminals achieve 100% Unicode compliance. Lagging: vt100.js (38%), Kitty (63%), Terminal.app (63%). Most commonly missing: Variation selector 16 (emoji presentation), Emoji ZWJ sequences (2 cols), Grapheme Cluster Cursor Movement.

compare/ghostty-vs-kitty.analysis: Ghostty scores 91% (231/254) vs Kitty at 86% (218/254). Ghostty leads by 5 percentage points. Only in Ghostty: Kitty keyboard protocol, Kitty keyboard: DISAMBIGUATE (flag 1), Kitty keyboard: REPORT_EVENTS (flag 2), Kitty keyboard: REPORT_ALTERNATE (flag 4), Kitty keyboard: REPORT_ALL_KEYS (flag 8) and 14 more. Only in Kitty: Kitty graphics: Unicode placeholders, Sixel graphics, Special color (OSC 5), Highlight background (OSC 17), Highlight foreground (OSC 19) and 1 more.

compare/ghostty-vs-iterm2.analysis: Ghostty scores 91% (231/254) vs iTerm2 at 94% (238/254). iTerm2 leads by 3 percentage points. Only in Ghostty: Report cell size in pixels (CSI 16 t). Only in iTerm2: OSC 52 clipboard read, Highlight background (OSC 17), Highlight foreground (OSC 19), Sixel support in DA1, Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3) and 3 more.

compare/ghostty-vs-warp.analysis: Ghostty scores 91% (231/254) vs Warp at 86% (218/254). Ghostty leads by 5 percentage points. Only in Ghostty: Color palette (OSC 4), Cursor back (CUB), Cursor down (CUD), Cursor up (CUU), Variation selector 16 (emoji presentation) and 9 more. Only in Warp: CUP with DECOM.

compare/ghostty-vs-terminal-app.analysis: Ghostty scores 91% (231/254) vs Terminal.app at 84% (214/254). Ghostty leads by 7 percentage points. Only in Ghostty: Kitty keyboard protocol, Kitty keyboard: DISAMBIGUATE (flag 1), Kitty keyboard: REPORT_EVENTS (flag 2), Kitty keyboard: REPORT_ALTERNATE (flag 4), Kitty keyboard: REPORT_ALL_KEYS (flag 8) and 18 more. Only in Terminal.app: CUP at screen boundaries, Kitty graphics: Unicode placeholders, Sixel graphics, Special color (OSC 5), Highlight background (OSC 17) and 1 more.

compare/iterm2-vs-kitty.analysis: iTerm2 scores 94% (238/254) vs Kitty at 86% (218/254). iTerm2 leads by 8 percentage points. Only in iTerm2: Kitty keyboard protocol, Kitty keyboard: DISAMBIGUATE (flag 1), Kitty keyboard: REPORT_EVENTS (flag 2), Kitty keyboard: REPORT_ALTERNATE (flag 4), Kitty keyboard: REPORT_ALL_KEYS (flag 8) and 19 more. Only in Kitty: Kitty graphics: Unicode placeholders, Sixel graphics, Special color (OSC 5), CUP at screen boundaries.

compare/kitty-vs-warp.analysis: Kitty scores 86% (218/254) vs Warp at 86% (218/254). Both terminals are tied in overall score. Only in Kitty: Kitty graphics: Unicode placeholders, Sixel graphics, Color palette (OSC 4), Special color (OSC 5), Highlight background (OSC 17) and 6 more. Only in Warp: ANSI save cursor (CSI s), ANSI restore cursor (CSI u), CUP with DECOM, Origin mode (DECOM), Terminal Version (XTVERSION) and 6 more.

compare/iterm2-vs-terminal-app.analysis: iTerm2 scores 94% (238/254) vs Terminal.app at 84% (214/254). iTerm2 leads by 10 percentage points. Only in iTerm2: Kitty keyboard protocol, Kitty keyboard: DISAMBIGUATE (flag 1), Kitty keyboard: REPORT_EVENTS (flag 2), Kitty keyboard: REPORT_ALTERNATE (flag 4), Kitty keyboard: REPORT_ALL_KEYS (flag 8) and 23 more. Only in Terminal.app: CUP at screen boundaries, Kitty graphics: Unicode placeholders, Sixel graphics, Special color (OSC 5).

compare/iterm2-vs-warp.analysis: iTerm2 scores 94% (238/254) vs Warp at 86% (218/254). iTerm2 leads by 8 percentage points. Only in iTerm2: OSC 52 clipboard read, Color palette (OSC 4), Highlight background (OSC 17), Highlight foreground (OSC 19), Sixel support in DA1 and 16 more. Only in Warp: CUP with DECOM.

compare/cursor-vs-vs-code.analysis: Cursor scores 85% (216/254) vs VS Code at 88% (223/254). VS Code leads by 3 percentage points. Only in VS Code: Terminal Version (XTVERSION), Kitty keyboard protocol, Kitty keyboard: DISAMBIGUATE (flag 1), Kitty keyboard: REPORT_EVENTS (flag 2), Kitty keyboard: REPORT_ALTERNATE (flag 4) and 2 more.

compare/terminal-app-vs-warp.analysis: Terminal.app scores 84% (214/254) vs Warp at 86% (218/254). Warp leads by 2 percentage points. Only in Terminal.app: CUP at screen boundaries, Kitty graphics: Unicode placeholders, Sixel graphics, Color palette (OSC 4), Special color (OSC 5) and 2 more. Only in Warp: ANSI save cursor (CSI s), ANSI restore cursor (CSI u), CUP with DECOM, Origin mode (DECOM), Terminal Version (XTVERSION) and 6 more.

sgr.analysis: The SGR (Text Styling) category covers 32 features. Top performers (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Warp and 6 more. Common gaps: Faint/dim (SGR 2) (1 terminal fails), Italic (SGR 3) (1 terminal fails), Double underline (SGR 21) (1 terminal fails).

cursor.analysis: The Cursor category covers 22 features. Top performers (100%): vterm.js, xterm.js. Common gaps: CUP at screen boundaries (6 terminals fail), CUP with DECOM (5 terminals fail), Cursor up (CUU) (4 terminals fail).

text.analysis: The Text category covers 20 features. Top performers (100%): vterm.js. Common gaps: TBC clear tab stop (CSI g) (11 terminals fail), Variation selector 16 (emoji presentation) (9 terminals fail), CHT forward tab (CSI I) (8 terminals fail).

erase.analysis: The Erase category covers 11 features. Top performers (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Warp and 4 more. Common gaps: Selective Erase (DECSED) (3 terminals fail), EL erases with bg color (2 terminals fail), Erase character (ECH) (1 terminal fails).

editing.analysis: The Editing category covers 17 features. Common gaps: DECRQCRA — checksum rectangular area (12 terminals fail), SL — shift left (CSI Ps SP @) (5 terminals fail), SR — shift right (CSI Ps SP A) (5 terminals fail).

modes.analysis: The Modes category covers 30 features. Top performers (100%): Ghostty, iTerm2, WezTerm. Common gaps: Mode 2031 color scheme reporting (8 terminals fail), Save/restore cursor (?1048) (4 terminals fail), Origin mode (DECOM) (2 terminals fail).

scrollback.analysis: The Scrollback category covers 9 features. Top performers (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Warp and 6 more. Common gaps: Alt screen separate scrollback (1 terminal fails).

reset.analysis: The Reset category covers 5 features. Top performers (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Cursor, VS Code and 4 more. Common gaps: DECALN screen alignment (ESC # 8) (3 terminals fail), Soft reset (DECSTR) (1 terminal fails).

extensions.analysis: The Extensions category covers 79 features. Top performers (100%): vterm.js. Common gaps: iTerm2 Cell Size Reporting (OSC 1337) (11 terminals fail), iTerm2 Capability Reporting (OSC 1337) (11 terminals fail), OSC 5522 advanced clipboard (11 terminals fail).

charsets.analysis: The Character Sets category covers 4 features. All 12 tested terminals achieve 100%.

device.analysis: The Device Status category covers 28 features. Top performers (100%): vterm.js. Common gaps: XTREPORTCOLORS — report color capabilities (CSI # R) (10 terminals fail), XTGETXRES — query resource value (DCS + Q) (10 terminals fail), Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3) (8 terminals fail).

input.analysis: The Input Protocols category covers 8 features. Top performers (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Warp and 3 more. Common gaps: urxvt Mouse Reporting (1015) (4 terminals fail), X10 Mouse Tracking (9) (3 terminals fail), modifyOtherKeys (2 terminals fail).

unicode.analysis: Unicode defines 10 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 77%. Full compliance (100%): Ghostty, iTerm2, vterm.js. Lowest: vt100.js at 40% (4/10).

ecma-48.analysis: ECMA-48 Standard defines 73 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 92%. Full compliance (100%): vterm.js. Lowest: vt100.js at 68% (50/73).

vt100.analysis: VT100 defines 57 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 93%. Lowest: vt100.js at 86% (49/57).

vt220.analysis: VT220 defines 4 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 88%. Full compliance (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Warp and 4 more. Lowest: vt100.js at 0% (0/4).

vt510.analysis: VT510 defines 20 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 71%. Lowest: vt100.js at 28% (5/18).

dec-private-modes.analysis: DEC Private Modes defines 30 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 91%. Full compliance (100%): Cursor, VS Code. Lowest: vt100.js at 54% (15/28).

xterm-extensions.analysis: Xterm Extensions defines 81 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 78%. Lowest: vt100.js at 32% (24/76).

kitty-extensions.analysis: Kitty Extensions defines 26 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 68%. Full compliance (100%): vterm.js. Lowest: vt100.js at 0% (0/21).

iterm2.analysis: iTerm2 Extensions defines 4 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 11%. Full compliance (100%): vterm.js. Lowest: xterm.js at 0% (0/2).

conemu.analysis: ConEmu Extensions defines 1 feature in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 100%. Full compliance (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Warp and 2 more.

vscode-extensions.analysis: VS Code Extensions defines 7 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 97%. Full compliance (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Warp and 4 more. Lowest: xterm.js at 86% (6/7).

osc.analysis: Operating System Commands (OSC) defines 61 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 78%. Full compliance (100%): vterm.js. Lowest: vt100.js at 41% (17/41).

sixel.analysis: Sixel Graphics defines 3 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 28%. Full compliance (100%): vterm.js. Lowest: Alacritty at 0% (0/3).

framework/silvery.analysis: Silvery requires the Modern TUI baseline — 2 of 12 tested terminals are fully compatible. Compatible: Ghostty, iTerm2. Same baseline tier (Modern TUI) as Textual, Bubbletea, Ratatui. Other frameworks target: Ink, Blessed (Core TUI).

framework/ink.analysis: Ink requires the Core TUI baseline — 1 of 12 tested terminals are fully compatible. Compatible: vterm.js. Same baseline tier (Core TUI) as Blessed. Other frameworks target: Silvery, Textual, Bubbletea, Ratatui (Modern TUI).

framework/textual.analysis: Textual requires the Modern TUI baseline — 2 of 12 tested terminals are fully compatible. Compatible: Ghostty, iTerm2. Same baseline tier (Modern TUI) as Silvery, Bubbletea, Ratatui. Other frameworks target: Ink, Blessed (Core TUI).

framework/bubbletea.analysis: Bubbletea requires the Modern TUI baseline — 2 of 12 tested terminals are fully compatible. Compatible: Ghostty, iTerm2. Same baseline tier (Modern TUI) as Silvery, Textual, Ratatui. Other frameworks target: Ink, Blessed (Core TUI).

framework/ratatui.analysis: Ratatui requires the Modern TUI baseline — 2 of 12 tested terminals are fully compatible. Compatible: Ghostty, iTerm2. Same baseline tier (Modern TUI) as Silvery, Textual, Bubbletea. Other frameworks target: Ink, Blessed (Core TUI).

framework/blessed.analysis: Blessed requires the Core TUI baseline — 1 of 12 tested terminals are fully compatible. Compatible: vterm.js. Same baseline tier (Core TUI) as Ink. Other frameworks target: Silvery, Textual, Bubbletea, Ratatui (Modern TUI).

standards-index.analysis: Terminfo.dev tracks 13 terminal standards, from ECMA-48 (1976) to Kitty Extensions (2017). Highest average adoption: ConEmu Extensions at 100%. Lowest: iTerm2 Extensions at 11%. 1 of 12 terminals achieve 100% ECMA-48 Standard compliance.

features-index.analysis: The terminfo.dev matrix covers 269 features across 13 categories. Best-supported category: Character Sets (100% average). Most challenging: Device Status (63%). 119 features are universally supported by all tested terminals. 1 feature has zero support across all tested terminals.
